message:
perf(vdbe): promote AddImm into try_execute_hot_opcode hot-path

AddImm is the canonical counter/accumulator increment — emitted in
COUNT(*) row counters, aggregate folding, recursive-CTE step counters,
and various conformance hot loops. Previously it fell through the hot
pre-filter into the 190-arm main `match op.opcode` in
`execute_with_borrowed_bindings_internal`, paying the full
jump-through-all-arms cost on every iteration.

The hot-path arm is a read + `wrapping_add` + `set_reg_int`; the register
almost always already holds `Integer`, so the body is cheap and the win
is purely in dispatch shortening.

Before/after on the VDBE dispatch micro-bench (criterion, p<0.05):

  vdbe_pipeline_execute/64    872.59 ns   ->  781.14 ns  (+11% thrpt, within noise on /64)
  vdbe_pipeline_execute/256   3.1150 us   ->  2.8355 us  (+9.9% thrpt, p=0.00)
  vdbe_pipeline_execute/1024  12.424 us   ->  11.334 us  (+9.5% thrpt, p=0.00)

Bench: `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cc2_vdbe_perf \
  cargo bench -p fsqlite-vdbe --bench pipeline_stages -- \
  vdbe_pipeline_execute --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 4`

Behaviour is byte-equivalent to the original arm (kept intact for
correctness; `#[allow(unreachable_patterns)]` on the main match already
tolerates the duplicate). `test_add_imm`, `test_add_imm_negative`, and
fsqlite-vdbe lib unit tests unchanged on the added-code path.

Bead: bd-1dp9.6.2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
